# SecretRotationValidator.ps1
# Module for validating secret rotation status and generating reports.
#
# Approach:
#   1. Get-SecretStatus   - Classifies a single secret as Expired/Warning/OK
#   2. Get-RotationReport - Processes multiple secrets and groups by urgency
#   3. ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown - Renders a report as a markdown table
#   4. ConvertTo-RotationJson    - Renders a report as JSON
#   5. Import-SecretsConfig      - Loads secrets from a JSON config file
#   6. Format-RotationOutput     - Dispatches to the correct formatter

function Get-SecretStatus {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Classifies a secret as Expired, Warning, or OK based on its rotation policy.
    .PARAMETER Secret
        A hashtable with Name, LastRotated (datetime), RotationDays (int), RequiredBy (string[]).
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against (defaults to now).
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Number of days before expiry to trigger a warning (default 14).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$Secret,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Calculate when the secret expires and how many days remain
    $expiryDate = ([datetime]$Secret.LastRotated).AddDays($Secret.RotationDays)
    $daysUntilExpiry = ($expiryDate - $ReferenceDate).Days

    # Determine status based on days until expiry relative to warning window
    if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) {
        $status = "Expired"
    }
    elseif ($daysUntilExpiry -le $WarningDays) {
        $status = "Warning"
    }
    else {
        $status = "OK"
    }

    # Build and return the result object
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Name            = $Secret.Name
        LastRotated     = [datetime]$Secret.LastRotated
        RotationDays    = $Secret.RotationDays
        ExpiryDate      = $expiryDate
        DaysUntilExpiry = $daysUntilExpiry
        DaysOverdue     = if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) { [Math]::Abs($daysUntilExpiry) } else { 0 }
        Status          = $status
        RequiredBy      = $Secret.RequiredBy
    }
}

function Get-RotationReport {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Generates a rotation report grouping secrets by urgency.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Classify each secret
    $results = foreach ($secret in $Secrets) {
        Get-SecretStatus -Secret $secret -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays
    }

    # Group by status — ensure arrays even when empty
    $expired = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Expired" } | Sort-Object DaysOverdue -Descending)
    $warning = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Warning" } | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)
    $ok      = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "OK" }      | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)

    # Build summary
    $summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
        TotalSecrets = $Secrets.Count
        ExpiredCount = $expired.Count
        WarningCount = $warning.Count
        OKCount      = $ok.Count
        ReportDate   = $ReferenceDate
        WarningDays  = $WarningDays
    }

    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Expired = $expired
        Warning = $warning
        OK      = $ok
        Summary = $summary
    }
}

function ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to a markdown-formatted string with tables.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("# Secret Rotation Report")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Report Date:** $($Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd'))")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Warning Window:** $($Report.Summary.WarningDays) days")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Summary section
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Summary")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Metric | Count |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Total Secrets | $($Report.Summary.TotalSecrets) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Expired | $($Report.Summary.ExpiredCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Warning | $($Report.Summary.WarningCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| OK | $($Report.Summary.OKCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Detail table with all secrets
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Details")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Name | Status | Last Rotated | Rotation Policy | Days Until Expiry | Required By |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |")

    # Emit expired first, then warning, then ok
    $allEntries = @($Report.Expired) + @($Report.Warning) + @($Report.OK)
    foreach ($entry in $allEntries) {
        $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
        $daysDisplay = if ($entry.Status -eq "Expired") { "$($entry.DaysOverdue) overdue" } else { $entry.DaysUntilExpiry }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($entry.Name) | $($entry.Status) | $($entry.LastRotated.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')) | $($entry.RotationDays) days | $daysDisplay | $services |")
    }

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Notifications section grouped by urgency
    if ($Report.Expired.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Expired Secrets (Immediate Action Required)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Expired) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: $($entry.DaysOverdue) days overdue, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    if ($Report.Warning.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Warning (Rotation Needed Soon)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Warning) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: expires in $($entry.DaysUntilExpiry) days, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    $sb.ToString()
}

function ConvertTo-RotationJson {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to JSON format.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    # Build a clean structure suitable for JSON serialization
    $jsonObj = @{
        summary = @{
            totalSecrets = $Report.Summary.TotalSecrets
            expiredCount = $Report.Summary.ExpiredCount
            warningCount = $Report.Summary.WarningCount
            okCount      = $Report.Summary.OKCount
            reportDate   = $Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
            warningDays  = $Report.Summary.WarningDays
        }
        expired = @(foreach ($e in $Report.Expired) {
            @{
                name            = $e.Name
                lastRotated     = $e.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $e.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $e.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysOverdue     = $e.DaysOverdue
                daysUntilExpiry = $e.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $e.Status
                requiredBy      = @($e.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        warning = @(foreach ($w in $Report.Warning) {
            @{
                name            = $w.Name
                lastRotated     = $w.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $w.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $w.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $w.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $w.Status
                requiredBy      = @($w.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        ok = @(foreach ($o in $Report.OK) {
            @{
                name            = $o.Name
                lastRotated     = $o.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $o.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $o.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $o.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $o.Status
                requiredBy      = @($o.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
    }

    $jsonObj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
}

function Import-SecretsConfig {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Loads secret configuration from a JSON file and returns normalized hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Path
        Path to the JSON configuration file.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Path
    )

    # Validate the file exists
    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
        throw "Configuration file not found: $Path"
    }

    # Read and parse the JSON
    try {
        $raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
        $config = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    }
    catch [System.ArgumentException] {
        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path"
    }
    catch {
        # Re-throw with a clearer message for any JSON parse error
        if ($_.Exception.Message -match "ConvertFrom-Json" -or $_.Exception.Message -match "Json") {
            throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path"
        }
        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path - $($_.Exception.Message)"
    }

    # Normalize each secret entry into the expected hashtable format
    $secrets = foreach ($s in $config.secrets) {
        @{
            Name         = $s.name
            LastRotated  = [datetime]::Parse($s.lastRotated)
            RotationDays = [int]$s.rotationDays
            RequiredBy   = @($s.requiredBy)
        }
    }

    @($secrets)
}

function Format-RotationOutput {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        High-level function: generates a report and formats it in the requested format.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Format
        Output format: "markdown" or "json".
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        Date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Format,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    $report = Get-RotationReport -Secrets $Secrets -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays

    switch ($Format.ToLower()) {
        "markdown" { ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown -Report $report }
        "json"     { ConvertTo-RotationJson -Report $report }
        default    { throw "Unsupported output format: $Format. Supported formats: markdown, json" }
    }
}

            LastRotated  = [datetime]::Parse($s.lastRotated)
            RotationDays = [int]$s.rotationDays
            RequiredBy   = @($s.requiredBy)
        }
    }

    @($secrets)
}

function Format-RotationOutput {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        High-level function: generates a report and formats it in the requested format.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Format
        Output format: "markdown" or "json".
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        Date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Format,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    $report = Get-RotationReport -Secrets $Secrets -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays

    switch ($Format.ToLower()) {
        "markdown" { ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown -Report $report }
        "json"     { ConvertTo-RotationJson -Report $report }
        default    { throw "Unsupported output format: $Format. Supported formats: markdown, json" }
    }
}

1→# SecretRotationValidator.ps1
     2→# Module for validating secret rotation status and generating reports.
     3→#
     4→# Approach:
     5→#   1. Get-SecretStatus   - Classifies a single secret as Expired/Warning/OK
     6→#   2. Get-RotationReport - Processes multiple secrets and groups by urgency
     7→#   3. ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown - Renders a report as a markdown table
     8→#   4. ConvertTo-RotationJson    - Renders a report as JSON
     9→#   5. Import-SecretsConfig      - Loads secrets from a JSON config file
    10→#   6. Format-RotationOutput     - Dispatches to the correct formatter
    11→
    12→function Get-SecretStatus {
    13→    <#
    14→    .SYNOPSIS
    15→        Classifies a secret as Expired, Warning, or OK based on its rotation policy.
    16→    .PARAMETER Secret
    17→        A hashtable with Name, LastRotated (datetime), RotationDays (int), RequiredBy (string[]).
    18→    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
    19→        The date to evaluate against (defaults to now).
    20→    .PARAMETER WarningDays
    21→        Number of days before expiry to trigger a warning (default 14).
    22→    #>
    23→    [CmdletBinding()]
    24→    param(
    25→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    26→        [hashtable]$Secret,
    27→
    28→        [Parameter()]
    29→        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),
    30→
    31→        [Parameter()]
    32→        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    33→    )
    34→
    35→    # Calculate when the secret expires and how many days remain
    36→    $expiryDate = ([datetime]$Secret.LastRotated).AddDays($Secret.RotationDays)
    37→    $daysUntilExpiry = ($expiryDate - $ReferenceDate).Days
    38→
    39→    # Determine status based on days until expiry relative to warning window
    40→    if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) {
    41→        $status = "Expired"
    42→    }
    43→    elseif ($daysUntilExpiry -le $WarningDays) {
    44→        $status = "Warning"
    45→    }
    46→    else {
    47→        $status = "OK"
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    # Build and return the result object
    51→    [PSCustomObject]@{
    52→        Name            = $Secret.Name
    53→        LastRotated     = [datetime]$Secret.LastRotated
    54→        RotationDays    = $Secret.RotationDays
    55→        ExpiryDate      = $expiryDate
    56→        DaysUntilExpiry = $daysUntilExpiry
    57→        DaysOverdue     = if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) { [Math]::Abs($daysUntilExpiry) } else { 0 }
    58→        Status          = $status
    59→        RequiredBy      = $Secret.RequiredBy
    60→    }
    61→}
    62→
    63→function Get-RotationReport {
    64→    <#
    65→    .SYNOPSIS
    66→        Generates a rotation report grouping secrets by urgency.
    67→    .PARAMETER Secrets
    68→        Array of secret hashtables.
    69→    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
    70→        The date to evaluate against.
    71→    .PARAMETER WarningDays
    72→        Warning window in days.
    73→    #>
    74→    [CmdletBinding()]
    75→    param(
    76→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    77→        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
    78→        [array]$Secrets,
    79→
    80→        [Parameter()]
    81→        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),
    82→
    83→        [Parameter()]
    84→        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    85→    )
    86→
    87→    # Classify each secret
    88→    $results = foreach ($secret in $Secrets) {
    89→        Get-SecretStatus -Secret $secret -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    # Group by status — ensure arrays even when empty
    93→    $expired = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Expired" } | Sort-Object DaysOverdue -Descending)
    94→    $warning = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Warning" } | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)
    95→    $ok      = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "OK" }      | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)
    96→
    97→    # Build summary
    98→    $summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
    99→        TotalSecrets = $Secrets.Count
   100→        ExpiredCount = $expired.Count
   101→        WarningCount = $warning.Count
   102→        OKCount      = $ok.Count
   103→        ReportDate   = $ReferenceDate
   104→        WarningDays  = $WarningDays
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    [PSCustomObject]@{
   108→        Expired = $expired
   109→        Warning = $warning
   110→        OK      = $ok
   111→        Summary = $summary
   112→    }
   113→}
   114→
   115→function ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown {
   116→    <#
   117→    .SYNOPSIS
   118→        Converts a rotation report to a markdown-formatted string with tables.
   119→    #>
   120→    [CmdletBinding()]
   121→    param(
   122→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   123→        [PSCustomObject]$Report
   124→    )
   125→
   126→    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
   127→
   128→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("# Secret Rotation Report")
   129→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   130→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Report Date:** $($Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd'))")
   131→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Warning Window:** $($Report.Summary.WarningDays) days")
   132→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   133→
   134→    # Summary section
   135→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Summary")
   136→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   137→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Metric | Count |")
   138→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- |")
   139→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Total Secrets | $($Report.Summary.TotalSecrets) |")
   140→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Expired | $($Report.Summary.ExpiredCount) |")
   141→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Warning | $($Report.Summary.WarningCount) |")
   142→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| OK | $($Report.Summary.OKCount) |")
   143→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   144→
   145→    # Detail table with all secrets
   146→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Details")
   147→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   148→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Name | Status | Last Rotated | Rotation Policy | Days Until Expiry | Required By |")
   149→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |")
   150→
   151→    # Emit expired first, then warning, then ok
   152→    $allEntries = @($Report.Expired) + @($Report.Warning) + @($Report.OK)
   153→    foreach ($entry in $allEntries) {
   154→        $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
   155→        $daysDisplay = if ($entry.Status -eq "Expired") { "$($entry.DaysOverdue) overdue" } else { $entry.DaysUntilExpiry }
   156→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($entry.Name) | $($entry.Status) | $($entry.LastRotated.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')) | $($entry.RotationDays) days | $daysDisplay | $services |")
   157→    }
   158→
   159→    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   160→
   161→    # Notifications section grouped by urgency
   162→    if ($Report.Expired.Count -gt 0) {
   163→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Expired Secrets (Immediate Action Required)")
   164→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   165→        foreach ($entry in $Report.Expired) {
   166→            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
   167→            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: $($entry.DaysOverdue) days overdue, affects: $services")
   168→        }
   169→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    if ($Report.Warning.Count -gt 0) {
   173→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Warning (Rotation Needed Soon)")
   174→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   175→        foreach ($entry in $Report.Warning) {
   176→            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
   177→            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: expires in $($entry.DaysUntilExpiry) days, affects: $services")
   178→        }
   179→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
   180→    }
   181→
   182→    $sb.ToString()
   183→}
   184→
   185→function ConvertTo-RotationJson {
   186→    <#
   187→    .SYNOPSIS
   188→        Converts a rotation report to JSON format.
   189→    #>
   190→    [CmdletBinding()]
   191→    param(
   192→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   193→        [PSCustomObject]$Report
   194→    )
   195→
   196→    # Build a clean structure suitable for JSON serialization
   197→    $jsonObj = @{
   198→        summary = @{
   199→            totalSecrets = $Report.Summary.TotalSecrets
   200→            expiredCount = $Report.Summary.ExpiredCount
   201→            warningCount = $Report.Summary.WarningCount
   202→            okCount      = $Report.Summary.OKCount
   203→            reportDate   = $Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   204→            warningDays  = $Report.Summary.WarningDays
   205→        }
   206→        expired = @(foreach ($e in $Report.Expired) {
   207→            @{
   208→                name            = $e.Name
   209→                lastRotated     = $e.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   210→                rotationDays    = $e.RotationDays
   211→                expiryDate      = $e.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   212→                daysOverdue     = $e.DaysOverdue
   213→                daysUntilExpiry = $e.DaysUntilExpiry
   214→                status          = $e.Status
   215→                requiredBy      = @($e.RequiredBy)
   216→            }
   217→        })
   218→        warning = @(foreach ($w in $Report.Warning) {
   219→            @{
   220→                name            = $w.Name
   221→                lastRotated     = $w.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   222→                rotationDays    = $w.RotationDays
   223→                expiryDate      = $w.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   224→                daysUntilExpiry = $w.DaysUntilExpiry
   225→                status          = $w.Status
   226→                requiredBy      = @($w.RequiredBy)
   227→            }
   228→        })
   229→        ok = @(foreach ($o in $Report.OK) {
   230→            @{
   231→                name            = $o.Name
   232→                lastRotated     = $o.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   233→                rotationDays    = $o.RotationDays
   234→                expiryDate      = $o.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
   235→                daysUntilExpiry = $o.DaysUntilExpiry
   236→                status          = $o.Status
   237→                requiredBy      = @($o.RequiredBy)
   238→            }
   239→        })
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    $jsonObj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
   243→}
   244→
   245→function Import-SecretsConfig {
   246→    <#
   247→    .SYNOPSIS
   248→        Loads secret configuration from a JSON file and returns normalized hashtables.
   249→    .PARAMETER Path
   250→        Path to the JSON configuration file.
   251→    #>
   252→    [CmdletBinding()]
   253→    param(
   254→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   255→        [string]$Path
   256→    )
   257→
   258→    # Validate the file exists
   259→    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
   260→        throw "Configuration file not found: $Path"
   261→    }
   262→
   263→    # Read and parse the JSON
   264→    try {
   265→        $raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
   266→        $config = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json
   267→    }
   268→    catch [System.ArgumentException] {
   269→        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path"
   270→    }
   271→    catch {
   272→        # Re-throw with a clearer message for any JSON parse error
   273→        if ($_.Exception.Message -match "ConvertFrom-Json" -or $_.Exception.Message -match "Json") {
   274→            throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path"
   275→        }
   276→        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path - $($_.Exception.Message)"
   277→    }
   278→
   279→    # Normalize each secret entry into the expected hashtable format
   280→    $secrets = foreach ($s in $config.secrets) {
   281→        @{
   282→            Name         = $s.name
   283→            LastRotated  = [datetime]::Parse($s.lastRotated)
   284→            RotationDays = [int]$s.rotationDays
   285→            RequiredBy   = @($s.requiredBy)
   286→        }
   287→    }
   288→
   289→    @($secrets)
   290→}
   291→
   292→function Format-RotationOutput {
   293→    <#
   294→    .SYNOPSIS
   295→        High-level function: generates a report and formats it in the requested format.
   296→    .PARAMETER Secrets
   297→        Array of secret hashtables.
   298→    .PARAMETER Format
   299→        Output format: "markdown" or "json".
   300→    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
   301→        Date to evaluate against.
   302→    .PARAMETER WarningDays
   303→        Warning window in days.
   304→    #>
   305→    [CmdletBinding()]
   306→    param(
   307→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   308→        [array]$Secrets,
   309→
   310→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   311→        [string]$Format,
   312→
   313→        [Parameter()]
   314→        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),
   315→
   316→        [Parameter()]

   318→    )

   321→

   324→        "json"     { ConvertTo-RotationJson -Report $report }

# SecretRotationValidator.ps1
# Module for validating secret rotation status and generating reports.
#
# Approach:
#   1. Get-SecretStatus   - Classifies a single secret as Expired/Warning/OK
#   2. Get-RotationReport - Processes multiple secrets and groups by urgency
#   3. ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown - Renders a report as a markdown table
#   4. ConvertTo-RotationJson    - Renders a report as JSON
#   5. Import-SecretsConfig      - Loads secrets from a JSON config file
#   6. Format-RotationOutput     - Dispatches to the correct formatter

function Get-SecretStatus {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Classifies a secret as Expired, Warning, or OK based on its rotation policy.
    .PARAMETER Secret
        A hashtable with Name, LastRotated (datetime), RotationDays (int), RequiredBy (string[]).
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against (defaults to now).
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Number of days before expiry to trigger a warning (default 14).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$Secret,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Calculate when the secret expires and how many days remain
    $expiryDate = ([datetime]$Secret.LastRotated).AddDays($Secret.RotationDays)
    $daysUntilExpiry = ($expiryDate - $ReferenceDate).Days

    # Determine status based on days until expiry relative to warning window
    if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) {
        $status = "Expired"
    }
    elseif ($daysUntilExpiry -le $WarningDays) {
        $status = "Warning"
    }
    else {
        $status = "OK"
    }

    # Build and return the result object
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Name            = $Secret.Name
        LastRotated     = [datetime]$Secret.LastRotated
        RotationDays    = $Secret.RotationDays
        ExpiryDate      = $expiryDate
        DaysUntilExpiry = $daysUntilExpiry
        DaysOverdue     = if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) { [Math]::Abs($daysUntilExpiry) } else { 0 }
        Status          = $status
        RequiredBy      = $Secret.RequiredBy
    }
}

function Get-RotationReport {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Generates a rotation report grouping secrets by urgency.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Classify each secret
    $results = foreach ($secret in $Secrets) {
        Get-SecretStatus -Secret $secret -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays
    }

    # Group by status — ensure arrays even when empty
    $expired = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Expired" } | Sort-Object DaysOverdue -Descending)
    $warning = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Warning" } | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)
    $ok      = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "OK" }      | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)

    # Build summary
    $summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
        TotalSecrets = $Secrets.Count
        ExpiredCount = $expired.Count
        WarningCount = $warning.Count
        OKCount      = $ok.Count
        ReportDate   = $ReferenceDate
        WarningDays  = $WarningDays
    }

    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Expired = $expired
        Warning = $warning
        OK      = $ok
        Summary = $summary
    }
}

function ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to a markdown-formatted string with tables.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("# Secret Rotation Report")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Report Date:** $($Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd'))")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Warning Window:** $($Report.Summary.WarningDays) days")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Summary section
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Summary")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Metric | Count |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Total Secrets | $($Report.Summary.TotalSecrets) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Expired | $($Report.Summary.ExpiredCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Warning | $($Report.Summary.WarningCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| OK | $($Report.Summary.OKCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Detail table with all secrets
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Details")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Name | Status | Last Rotated | Rotation Policy | Days Until Expiry | Required By |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |")

    # Emit expired first, then warning, then ok
    $allEntries = @($Report.Expired) + @($Report.Warning) + @($Report.OK)
    foreach ($entry in $allEntries) {
        $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
        $daysDisplay = if ($entry.Status -eq "Expired") { "$($entry.DaysOverdue) overdue" } else { $entry.DaysUntilExpiry }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($entry.Name) | $($entry.Status) | $($entry.LastRotated.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')) | $($entry.RotationDays) days | $daysDisplay | $services |")
    }

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Notifications section grouped by urgency
    if ($Report.Expired.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Expired Secrets (Immediate Action Required)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Expired) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: $($entry.DaysOverdue) days overdue, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    if ($Report.Warning.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Warning (Rotation Needed Soon)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Warning) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: expires in $($entry.DaysUntilExpiry) days, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    $sb.ToString()
}

function ConvertTo-RotationJson {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to JSON format.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    # Build a clean structure suitable for JSON serialization
    $jsonObj = @{
        summary = @{
            totalSecrets = $Report.Summary.TotalSecrets
            expiredCount = $Report.Summary.ExpiredCount
            warningCount = $Report.Summary.WarningCount
            okCount      = $Report.Summary.OKCount
            reportDate   = $Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
            warningDays  = $Report.Summary.WarningDays
        }
        expired = @(foreach ($e in $Report.Expired) {
            @{
                name            = $e.Name
                lastRotated     = $e.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $e.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $e.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysOverdue     = $e.DaysOverdue
                daysUntilExpiry = $e.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $e.Status
                requiredBy      = @($e.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        warning = @(foreach ($w in $Report.Warning) {
            @{
                name            = $w.Name
                lastRotated     = $w.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $w.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $w.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $w.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $w.Status
                requiredBy      = @($w.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        ok = @(foreach ($o in $Report.OK) {
            @{
                name            = $o.Name
                lastRotated     = $o.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $o.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $o.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $o.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $o.Status
                requiredBy      = @($o.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
    }

    $jsonObj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
}

function Import-SecretsConfig {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS

# SecretRotationValidator.ps1
# Module for validating secret rotation status and generating reports.
#
# Approach:
#   1. Get-SecretStatus   - Classifies a single secret as Expired/Warning/OK
#   2. Get-RotationReport - Processes multiple secrets and groups by urgency
#   3. ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown - Renders a report as a markdown table
#   4. ConvertTo-RotationJson    - Renders a report as JSON
#   5. Import-SecretsConfig      - Loads secrets from a JSON config file
#   6. Format-RotationOutput     - Dispatches to the correct formatter

function Get-SecretStatus {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Classifies a secret as Expired, Warning, or OK based on its rotation policy.
    .PARAMETER Secret
        A hashtable with Name, LastRotated (datetime), RotationDays (int), RequiredBy (string[]).
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against (defaults to now).
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Number of days before expiry to trigger a warning (default 14).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$Secret,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Calculate when the secret expires and how many days remain
    $expiryDate = ([datetime]$Secret.LastRotated).AddDays($Secret.RotationDays)
    $daysUntilExpiry = ($expiryDate - $ReferenceDate).Days

    # Determine status based on days until expiry relative to warning window
    if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) {
        $status = "Expired"
    }
    elseif ($daysUntilExpiry -le $WarningDays) {
        $status = "Warning"
    }
    else {
        $status = "OK"
    }

    # Build and return the result object
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Name            = $Secret.Name
        LastRotated     = [datetime]$Secret.LastRotated
        RotationDays    = $Secret.RotationDays
        ExpiryDate      = $expiryDate
        DaysUntilExpiry = $daysUntilExpiry
        DaysOverdue     = if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) { [Math]::Abs($daysUntilExpiry) } else { 0 }
        Status          = $status
        RequiredBy      = $Secret.RequiredBy
    }
}

function Get-RotationReport {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Generates a rotation report grouping secrets by urgency.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Classify each secret
    $results = foreach ($secret in $Secrets) {
        Get-SecretStatus -Secret $secret -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays
    }

    # Group by status — ensure arrays even when empty
    $expired = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Expired" } | Sort-Object DaysOverdue -Descending)
    $warning = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Warning" } | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)
    $ok      = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "OK" }      | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)

    # Build summary
    $summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
        TotalSecrets = $Secrets.Count
        ExpiredCount = $expired.Count
        WarningCount = $warning.Count
        OKCount      = $ok.Count
        ReportDate   = $ReferenceDate
        WarningDays  = $WarningDays
    }

    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Expired = $expired
        Warning = $warning
        OK      = $ok
        Summary = $summary
    }
}

function ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to a markdown-formatted string with tables.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("# Secret Rotation Report")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Report Date:** $($Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd'))")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Warning Window:** $($Report.Summary.WarningDays) days")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Summary section
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Summary")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Metric | Count |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Total Secrets | $($Report.Summary.TotalSecrets) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Expired | $($Report.Summary.ExpiredCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Warning | $($Report.Summary.WarningCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| OK | $($Report.Summary.OKCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Detail table with all secrets
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Details")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Name | Status | Last Rotated | Rotation Policy | Days Until Expiry | Required By |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |")

    # Emit expired first, then warning, then ok
    $allEntries = @($Report.Expired) + @($Report.Warning) + @($Report.OK)
    foreach ($entry in $allEntries) {
        $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
        $daysDisplay = if ($entry.Status -eq "Expired") { "$($entry.DaysOverdue) overdue" } else { $entry.DaysUntilExpiry }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($entry.Name) | $($entry.Status) | $($entry.LastRotated.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')) | $($entry.RotationDays) days | $daysDisplay | $services |")
    }

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Notifications section grouped by urgency
    if ($Report.Expired.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Expired Secrets (Immediate Action Required)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Expired) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: $($entry.DaysOverdue) days overdue, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    if ($Report.Warning.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Warning (Rotation Needed Soon)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Warning) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: expires in $($entry.DaysUntilExpiry) days, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    $sb.ToString()
}

function ConvertTo-RotationJson {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to JSON format.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    # Build a clean structure suitable for JSON serialization
    $jsonObj = @{
        summary = @{
            totalSecrets = $Report.Summary.TotalSecrets
            expiredCount = $Report.Summary.ExpiredCount
            warningCount = $Report.Summary.WarningCount
            okCount      = $Report.Summary.OKCount
            reportDate   = $Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
            warningDays  = $Report.Summary.WarningDays
        }
        expired = @(foreach ($e in $Report.Expired) {
            @{
                name            = $e.Name
                lastRotated     = $e.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $e.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $e.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysOverdue     = $e.DaysOverdue
                daysUntilExpiry = $e.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $e.Status
                requiredBy      = @($e.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        warning = @(foreach ($w in $Report.Warning) {
            @{
                name            = $w.Name
                lastRotated     = $w.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $w.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $w.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $w.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $w.Status
                requiredBy      = @($w.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        ok = @(foreach ($o in $Report.OK) {
            @{
                name            = $o.Name
                lastRotated     = $o.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $o.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $o.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $o.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $o.Status
                requiredBy      = @($o.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
    }

    $jsonObj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
}

function Import-SecretsConfig {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Loads secret configuration from a JSON file and returns normalized hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Path
        Path to the JSON configuration file.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Path
    )

    # Validate the file exists
    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
        throw "Configuration file not found: $Path"
    }

    # Read and parse the JSON
    try {
        $raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw
        $config = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    }
    catch [System.ArgumentException] {
        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path"
    }
    catch {
        # Re-throw with a clearer message for any JSON parse error
        if ($_.Exception.Message -match "ConvertFrom-Json" -or $_.Exception.Message -match "Json") {
            throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path"
        }
        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path - $($_.Exception.Message)"
    }

    # Normalize each secret entry into the expected hashtable format
    $secrets = foreach ($s in $config.secrets) {
        @{
            Name         = $s.name
            LastRotated  = [datetime]::Parse($s.lastRotated)
            RotationDays = [int]$s.rotationDays
            RequiredBy   = @($s.requiredBy)
        }
    }

    @($secrets)
}

function Format-RotationOutput {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        High-level function: generates a report and formats it in the requested format.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Format
        Output format: "markdown" or "json".
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        Date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Format,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    $report = Get-RotationReport -Secrets $Secrets -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays

    switch ($Format.ToLower()) {
        "markdown" { ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown -Report $report }
        "json"     { ConvertTo-RotationJson -Report $report }
        default    { throw "Unsupported output format: $Format. Supported formats: markdown, json" }
    }
}

# SecretRotationValidator.ps1
# Module for validating secret rotation status and generating reports.
#
# Approach:
#   1. Get-SecretStatus   - Classifies a single secret as Expired/Warning/OK
#   2. Get-RotationReport - Processes multiple secrets and groups by urgency
#   3. ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown - Renders a report as a markdown table
#   4. ConvertTo-RotationJson    - Renders a report as JSON
#   5. Import-SecretsConfig      - Loads secrets from a JSON config file
#   6. Format-RotationOutput     - Dispatches to the correct formatter

function Get-SecretStatus {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Classifies a secret as Expired, Warning, or OK based on its rotation policy.
    .PARAMETER Secret
        A hashtable with Name, LastRotated (datetime), RotationDays (int), RequiredBy (string[]).
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against (defaults to now).
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Number of days before expiry to trigger a warning (default 14).
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [hashtable]$Secret,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Calculate when the secret expires and how many days remain
    $expiryDate = ([datetime]$Secret.LastRotated).AddDays($Secret.RotationDays)
    $daysUntilExpiry = ($expiryDate - $ReferenceDate).Days

    # Determine status based on days until expiry relative to warning window
    if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) {
        $status = "Expired"
    }
    elseif ($daysUntilExpiry -le $WarningDays) {
        $status = "Warning"
    }
    else {
        $status = "OK"
    }

    # Build and return the result object
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Name            = $Secret.Name
        LastRotated     = [datetime]$Secret.LastRotated
        RotationDays    = $Secret.RotationDays
        ExpiryDate      = $expiryDate
        DaysUntilExpiry = $daysUntilExpiry
        DaysOverdue     = if ($daysUntilExpiry -le 0) { [Math]::Abs($daysUntilExpiry) } else { 0 }
        Status          = $status
        RequiredBy      = $Secret.RequiredBy
    }
}

function Get-RotationReport {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Generates a rotation report grouping secrets by urgency.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        The date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    # Classify each secret
    $results = foreach ($secret in $Secrets) {
        Get-SecretStatus -Secret $secret -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays
    }

    # Group by status — ensure arrays even when empty
    $expired = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Expired" } | Sort-Object DaysOverdue -Descending)
    $warning = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "Warning" } | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)
    $ok      = @($results | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq "OK" }      | Sort-Object DaysUntilExpiry)

    # Build summary
    $summary = [PSCustomObject]@{
        TotalSecrets = $Secrets.Count
        ExpiredCount = $expired.Count
        WarningCount = $warning.Count
        OKCount      = $ok.Count
        ReportDate   = $ReferenceDate
        WarningDays  = $WarningDays
    }

    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Expired = $expired
        Warning = $warning
        OK      = $ok
        Summary = $summary
    }
}

function ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to a markdown-formatted string with tables.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("# Secret Rotation Report")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Report Date:** $($Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd'))")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("**Warning Window:** $($Report.Summary.WarningDays) days")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Summary section
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Summary")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Metric | Count |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Total Secrets | $($Report.Summary.TotalSecrets) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Expired | $($Report.Summary.ExpiredCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Warning | $($Report.Summary.WarningCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| OK | $($Report.Summary.OKCount) |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Detail table with all secrets
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Details")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| Name | Status | Last Rotated | Rotation Policy | Days Until Expiry | Required By |")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |")

    # Emit expired first, then warning, then ok
    $allEntries = @($Report.Expired) + @($Report.Warning) + @($Report.OK)
    foreach ($entry in $allEntries) {
        $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
        $daysDisplay = if ($entry.Status -eq "Expired") { "$($entry.DaysOverdue) overdue" } else { $entry.DaysUntilExpiry }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("| $($entry.Name) | $($entry.Status) | $($entry.LastRotated.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')) | $($entry.RotationDays) days | $daysDisplay | $services |")
    }

    [void]$sb.AppendLine("")

    # Notifications section grouped by urgency
    if ($Report.Expired.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Expired Secrets (Immediate Action Required)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Expired) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: $($entry.DaysOverdue) days overdue, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    if ($Report.Warning.Count -gt 0) {
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("## Warning (Rotation Needed Soon)")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
        foreach ($entry in $Report.Warning) {
            $services = ($entry.RequiredBy -join ", ")
            [void]$sb.AppendLine("- **$($entry.Name)**: expires in $($entry.DaysUntilExpiry) days, affects: $services")
        }
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("")
    }

    $sb.ToString()
}

function ConvertTo-RotationJson {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Converts a rotation report to JSON format.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject]$Report
    )

    # Build a clean structure suitable for JSON serialization
    $jsonObj = @{
        summary = @{
            totalSecrets = $Report.Summary.TotalSecrets
            expiredCount = $Report.Summary.ExpiredCount
            warningCount = $Report.Summary.WarningCount
            okCount      = $Report.Summary.OKCount
            reportDate   = $Report.Summary.ReportDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
            warningDays  = $Report.Summary.WarningDays
        }
        expired = @(foreach ($e in $Report.Expired) {
            @{
                name            = $e.Name
                lastRotated     = $e.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $e.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $e.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysOverdue     = $e.DaysOverdue
                daysUntilExpiry = $e.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $e.Status
                requiredBy      = @($e.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        warning = @(foreach ($w in $Report.Warning) {
            @{
                name            = $w.Name
                lastRotated     = $w.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $w.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $w.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $w.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $w.Status
                requiredBy      = @($w.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
        ok = @(foreach ($o in $Report.OK) {
            @{
                name            = $o.Name
                lastRotated     = $o.LastRotated.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                rotationDays    = $o.RotationDays
                expiryDate      = $o.ExpiryDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
                daysUntilExpiry = $o.DaysUntilExpiry
                status          = $o.Status
                requiredBy      = @($o.RequiredBy)
            }
        })
    }

    $jsonObj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
}

function Import-SecretsConfig {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Loads secret configuration from a JSON file and returns normalized hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Path
        Path to the JSON configuration file.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Path
    )

    # Validate the file exists
    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
        throw "Configuration file not found: $Path"
    }

    # Read and parse the JSON — use ErrorAction Stop to ensure parse errors are caught
    try {
        $raw = Get-Content -Path $Path -Raw -ErrorAction Stop
        $config = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -ErrorAction Stop
    }
    catch {
        throw "Failed to parse configuration file: $Path - $($_.Exception.Message)"
    }

    # Normalize each secret entry into the expected hashtable format
    $secrets = foreach ($s in $config.secrets) {
        @{
            Name         = $s.name
            LastRotated  = [datetime]::Parse($s.lastRotated)
            RotationDays = [int]$s.rotationDays
            RequiredBy   = @($s.requiredBy)
        }
    }

    @($secrets)
}

function Format-RotationOutput {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        High-level function: generates a report and formats it in the requested format.
    .PARAMETER Secrets
        Array of secret hashtables.
    .PARAMETER Format
        Output format: "markdown" or "json".
    .PARAMETER ReferenceDate
        Date to evaluate against.
    .PARAMETER WarningDays
        Warning window in days.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [array]$Secrets,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Format,

        [Parameter()]
        [datetime]$ReferenceDate = (Get-Date),

        [Parameter()]
        [int]$WarningDays = 14
    )

    $report = Get-RotationReport -Secrets $Secrets -ReferenceDate $ReferenceDate -WarningDays $WarningDays

    switch ($Format.ToLower()) {
        "markdown" { ConvertTo-RotationMarkdown -Report $report }
        "json"     { ConvertTo-RotationJson -Report $report }
        default    { throw "Unsupported output format: $Format. Supported formats: markdown, json" }
    }
}
